priceUpdate: SOL is +7.0% in the last 24h and -3.4% over the last 7 days, while sentiment is still extreme fear. That keeps the setup tradable, but not stable yet. 24h volume is about $4.12B, which shows active trading. The key question is whether buyers keep rotating into Solana instead of back into BTC and ETH. Near-term direction depends on whether new product launches headlines turn into real usage (wallets, swaps, borrowing), led by Bitwise acquires Chorus One, potentially paving way for more staked.
pastWeek: SOL finished the week down 3.0%. The most discussed driver was Bitwise acquires Chorus One, potentially paving way for more staked ETFs (Cointelegraph), because it points to where new liquidity may concentrate.
thisWeek: The lead setup this week is Bitwise acquires Chorus One, potentially paving way for more staked ETFs, with traders now reacting to specific Solana headlines. Next up is Seeker is up 40% in 24 hours. These stories only matter if they drive measurable usage, borrowing, and trading depth. Watch wallet growth, swaps, and perps depth around new product launches first.
whatsHot: What's hot: ai agents, yield, new products are getting the highest share of Solana headlines right now.
